name: audit-website
description: Audit websites for SEO, technical, content, and security issues using squirrelscan CLI. Returns LLM-optimized reports with health scores, broken links, meta tag analysis, and actionable recommendations. Use when analyzing websites, debugging SEO issues, or checking site health.
capabilities: Crawl websites and analyze pages against 149+ audit rules
Generate health scores with category breakdowns (SEO, technical, content, security)
Detect broken links, missing meta tags, accessibility issues, and performance problems
Output LLM-optimized reports for AI-assisted debugging and fixes
Check for leaked secrets, security headers, and HTTPS usage
Audit against E-E-A-T guidelines and accessibility standards
author: squirrelscan
license: See LICENSE file in repository root
securitySummary: Legitimate website auditing tool. Static findings are false positives - scanner misidentified documentation examples and URL patterns as security threats. The skill uses network access for website crawling and external commands to invoke the squirrel CLI, which is expected and documented behavior.
valueStatement: Website auditing is complex and time-consuming. The squirrelscan CLI automates comprehensive audits across 149+ rules covering SEO, performance, accessibility, and security, generating actionable reports for AI agents.
userTitle: Audit websites for SEO and security issues
limitations: Requires squirrel CLI to be installed and accessible in PATH
Cannot audit password-protected or authentication-required pages
Does not perform dynamic JavaScript rendering analysis beyond basic browser emulation
Audit speed depends on website size and server response times

useCases.title: Debug technical SEO issues
useCases.description: Analyze a website to identify and fix technical SEO problems like missing meta tags, broken links, and crawlability issues.

useCases.title: Generate site health reports
useCases.description: Run comprehensive audits that output health scores and actionable recommendations for improving website quality.

useCases.title: Enable AI-powered website fixes
useCases.description: Provide AI assistants with structured audit data to autonomously identify and suggest fixes for website issues.

promptTemplates.title: Quick site audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a website audit on https://example.com using the audit-website skill and show me the health score and top issues.
promptTemplates.scenario: Basic audit request with default options

promptTemplates.title: Detailed SEO analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit https://example.com for SEO issues. Check meta tags, headings, and content structure. Show me the LLM-formatted report with specific failing rules.
promptTemplates.scenario: Detailed SEO-focused audit requesting specific categories

promptTemplates.title: Security and accessibility check
promptTemplates.prompt: Run a focused audit on https://example.com checking for security headers, leaked secrets, and accessibility issues. Report any problems found.

promptTemplates.title: Full site comparison audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit my staging site at https://staging.example.com and compare against the live site. Show me what issues exist on staging that don't exist on production.

bestPractices: Use --format llm for AI-optimized output that is 40% smaller than verbose formats
Run audits on live websites for accurate rendering and performance data
Save audit IDs to compare site health before and after changes
Use --max-pages limit for large sites to control crawl duration
antiPatterns: Do not use --format llm with the audit command directly (only with report)
Do not audit local development URLs expecting production-equivalent results
Do not skip --format llm when working with AI assistants (verbose output wastes tokens)
Do not forget to verify installation with squirrel --version before running audits

faq.question: What is the difference between squirrel audit and squirrel report?
faq.answer: The audit command crawls and analyzes a website, saving results to a database. The report command generates output from a previous audit. Use audit to start, then report to export results in different formats.

faq.question: Why should I use --format llm instead of other formats?
faq.answer: The LLM format is optimized for AI consumption - it is 40-70% smaller than verbose XML, reducing token usage while maintaining all structured data needed for analysis.

faq.question: How long does a typical website audit take?
faq.answer: Audit time depends on site size and server response time. Small sites (10-50 pages) complete in seconds. Large sites (200+ pages) may take several minutes. Use --verbose to see progress.

faq.question: What audit rules are checked?
faq.answer: The squirrelscan CLI checks 149+ rules across 20 categories including SEO, technical, performance, content, security, accessibility, E-E-A-T, crawlability, and schema validation.

faq.question: Can I audit password-protected pages?
faq.answer: The basic audit does not support authentication. For password-protected content, ensure the site allows guest access or use staging environments with similar content.

faq.question: How do I compare audit results over time?
faq.answer: Each audit gets a unique ID. Run audits, save the IDs, then use report with different IDs to compare health scores and issue counts between audits.
